overall.description: A computer-generated ship simulation showing a first-person view from a vessel's bridge, featuring active navigation monitors and radar while moving through open ocean waters under overcast, dimly lit conditions.

scene_state.functional_zone: ship bridge with navigation consoles overlooking open ocean

events.description: depth sounder monitor briefly displays orange seabed contour at bottom of screen indicating shallower water
depth sounder monitor shows sustained orange-brown bottom return suggesting approaching shallows or coastline

agents.ego_description: navigating the ship from the bridge while monitoring the rotating radar and electronic chart displays

entered.detail: navigation display showing yellow-green landmass on blue nautical chart
circular black radar screen with concentric range rings and rotating yellow sweep arm
vertical profile display showing water column depth with blue gradient
forward deck of vessel with metal railings and red-brown deck plates
choppy dark grey sea extending to horizon under overcast sky

segments.scene_description: The camera remains fixed inside a ship's bridge, looking out over the bow as the vessel moves steadily through open ocean. Three navigation monitors display active radar and chart data overhead, while the depth sounder periodically registers the seabed as the ship progresses through rough, dimly lit waters.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot maintains a steady first-person perspective from a ship's bridge as the vessel navigates through a vast, dimly lit ocean. It begins with a fixed view looking over the bow, where the ship cuts through rough, dark waters. As the vessel progresses, the focus remains on the interaction between the external environment and the internal bridge equipment, with three overhead navigation monitors continuously updating radar and chart data. The scene concludes as the depth sounder periodically registers the seabed, grounding the movement of the ship within the overcast maritime expanse.
